frs_context.description: Specifies current scanner interface
frs_context.notes: Upon change to CI_INTERFACE_TYPE, if the requested interface type differs from Current, then the User configuration for the specified interface is loaded into Current; no action is taken if the new value is the same as Current. Following scanner power cycle, the Current configuration is loaded from saved User configuration; if the User configuration file is not present, it is automatically generated. If the scanner is power cycled or CI_INTERFACE_TYPE is changed following modification of any configuration tag value without first saving the Current configuration to User, the changes are lost

frs_context.description: Enables use of RS232 CTS signal for flow control and/or scan control
frs_context.notes: When CTS flow control is enabled, CTS signal controls transmission of data to host. When disabled, scanner simply transmits to host regardless of any activity on CTS line. Scan control using CTS line can be enabled/disabled. When this option is enabled, CTS line must be active for scanner to read and transmit data. While CTS line is inactive, scanner remains in a host-disabled state (RS232_Xmit_Disable); following a successful label transmission, CTS signal must transition to inactive and then to active to enable scanning for next label. Magsl CTS scan control works the same as CTS scan control except the CTS toggle at the end of transmission is not required, and the scanner will therefore not be disabled waiting for the CTS toggle.

frs_context.description: Specifies format in which scanner sends character data
frs_context.notes: Used by the keyboard wedge and USB keyboard interfaces. 0 = CAPS LOCK Off, send character data in normal format. 1 = CAPS LOCK ON, send alpha characters in reverse case. Numeric characters are unaffected. 2 = SHIFT-LOCK mode, send character data in shifted case. This only applies to Wedge G. This is not supported by this product, so this setting results in a CAPS LOCK Off functionality. 3 = CAPS LOCK COMPENSATION mode. In this mode the scanner detects if the capslock key is pressed on the keyboards and shifts the characters as needed to send the label as printed. This only applies to USB keyboard. For other interface this setting results in a CAPS LOCK Off functionality.

frs_context.description: Specifies how scanner transmits ASCII control characters to host
frs_context.notes: Affects suffix and prefix characters. When disabled, only ASCII characters between 20H and 127H inclusive (space... delete) plus special characters 0DH (carriage return), 08H (backspace), 1BH (ESC), 09H (right tab) and 0BH (left tab) are transmitted. Used by the keyboard wedge and USB keyboard interfaces. 0 = Disable 1 = Enable transmission of control characters to host 2 = Send characters between 00H and 1FH according to special function-key mapping table (This is used to send keys that are not in normal ASCII set; a unique set is provided for each available scancode set) 3 = Send characters between 00H and 14H according to special function-key mapping table. Control characters that are NAK and above are sent according to the country keyboard table.

frs_context.description: Extends single byte host commands to allow another byte to perform the same command.
frs_context.notes: Allows some single byte host commands to be extended, so another byte can execute the same command. Thr value bytes for this configuration item uses the hex value of ASCII characters. In the examples below, the actual characters are used. The single byte commands that can be extended are as follows; Byte 1 (left most byte) (R)eset Byte 2 (E)able Byte 3 (D)isable Byte 4 not on (F)ile Bytes 5-8 are reserved Byte - 1| 2 |3 |4 |5|6|7|8 ------------------- Cmd - |R|E|D|F | | | | | ------------------- For example, if (R)eset was extended to W and (E)nble was extended to X and (D)isable was extended to Y and not on (F)ile was extended to Z, the configuration item would look as follows: Byte - |1| 2 |3 |4 |5|6|7|8 -------------------- Cmd - |W|X|Y|Z | | | | | ------------------- Now both R and W would reset the scanner, both E and X would enable the scanner both D and Y would disable the scanner and F and Z would perform a not on file indication. The service port command to perform the extensions mentioned above would look as follows: 001004DF5758595A No byte can be extended to NULL. A NULL in any of the REDF positions means that that command has no corresponding extended character. No byte can be extended to any character that is currently used for an existing host command. For example a B character cannot be used as a character to extend any of the characters. B is already defined as the beep host command. A byte used to extend a command cannot be used to extend another command. If a byte is re-used to extend a command, that byte is ignored. For example, if W is used to extend the R (reset byte), W cannot be used to extend the enable, disable or the not on file bytes.
frs_context.head: RS232 Parameters

Values: 0 - default, alt beep 1 - REWE special beep Bit 2 controls USB hung label recovery Values: 0 - off 1 - if comet does not indicate label transmitted before next heartbeat occurs, comet is reset (USB KBD waits multiple heartbeats due to slow label transmission) Bit 3- 7 reserved

frs_context.notes: Mode 2 applies to RS232 STD and RS232 WN only. If set to mode 2 in other interfaces, mode 0 will be assumed. Mode 1 - For scanner with intelligent integrated EAS, label transmission (and hence good read beep) is completed after EAS logic executes. Mode 2 - This setting will only work if CTS Scan control is enabled. Mode3 - When beeping after each output structure decoded, if multiple output structures, there is a delay after the beep has finished. The delay is equal to the good beep length or can be set by a ULE script.

frs_context.description: The time segments of a decoded label are required to be absent from the volume.
frs_context.notes: Specifies the number of frames or 25msec cycles that the segments are stored by the software before being discarded if a label has not been successfully decoded during the current label in volume session. The "Label in volume session" is defined as the number of cycles between when the label gone time is first started until the label gone cycle timer expires. The maximum amount of time before the label gone frame timer expiration processing takes place can be the configured number of cycles + 1. In normal usage the label gone cycle timer should always be set higher than 01. Due to the way the software handles the timer the actual timer value (when set to 1) is between 0 cycles and 1 cycle. Settings of 0 and 1 are generally used for testing purposes only.
frs_context.head: Double Read Control

frs_context.description: Determines the operating mode for mobile phone read.
frs_context.notes: TOGGLE - cell (state) phone reading toggles between cell reading and normal reading and is controlled by host command. TIMER - cell (state) reading is activated by a host command and operates for a prescribed period of time. Upon expiration of that time cell reading is deactivated. TRANSPARENT - from normal reading, cell (state) reading is automatically activated periodically for brief periods of time and is normally not obvious to the end user. When set to TRANSPARENT cell reading remains on regardless of host command.

Computed as current frame saturation level + 0.3% margin. Default settings Byte 0 - pixel saturation level units of 0.01 %: 0.5% Byte 1 - 2D decoding dwell time in 10msec: 600msec Byte 2 - maximum number of frames to stay in cell phone mode: 32 frames Byte 3 - 0== cell mode automatic exposure control off, 1= cell mode automatic exposure control ON

frs_context.description: Used to control the method of processing coupon labels.
frs_context.notes: UPC/EAN labels that start with a '5' are defined as coupon labels. RSS Expanded labels that start with '8110' are defined as coupon labels. When this configuration item is set to 1, UPCA coupon labels will decode but RSS/DataBar Expanded coupon labels will not. RSS/DataBar Expanded labels that are not coupon labels will decode and all UPCA labels will decode. When this configuration items is set to 2, RSS/DataBar Expanded coupon labels will decode but .UPCA coupon labels will not. UPCA labels that are not coupon labels will decode and all RSS/DataBar Expanded labels will decode. When this configuration item is set to 3, UPCA coupon labels will be given priority. When this configuration item is set to 4, DataBar coupon labels will be given priority.NOTE:

frs_context.description: Specifies CI_1D_VL_TIME override on a per IPE basis.
frs_context.notes: Override the CI_1D_VL_TIME value on a per IPE basis. The CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP is used to associate a value with an IPE. Each value is two bytes, big endian. If the selected entry's value is zero the CI_1D_VL_TIME value is used. The default mapping index is: 0 = Vertical, 1 = Horizontal, 2 = 2D Processor, 3 = reserved - do not use The index into this array is defined as the contents of CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15] Example Array[CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15]] entry is used I.e. CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15]=1 then this array[1] controls the setting.
frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: Specifies a FIR filter
frs_context.notes: Specs locator line filter for vert imager. Payload format follows: Config payload=16 short int elemnts ea element=2 bytes=4 hex alphanumerics=16 bits 1st elemnt specs # of filter taps sent. A value 00-15(dec) is valid Last 15 elemts are populated with values or 00 for unused 16 elemnts must be sent even if < than 15 are used. A total of 64 hex values must be sent An endian mismatch exists between the FRS and POS sending a message, a byte swap is done for each elemnt of the payload Format ex. The user chooses 9 tap filter w following dec values. 2405 1103 -7360 7873 24726 7873 -7360 1103 2405 Converted to hex 0965 044F 9CC0 1EC1 6096 1EC1 9CC0 044F 0965 Hex vals must be byte swapped 6509 4F04 C09C C11E 9660 C11E C09C 4F04 6509 The payload is now built remembering that the 1st entry (number of coefficients) must also be swapped, and the last 6 unused elements padded w zeros 0900 6509 4F04 C09C C11E 9660 C11E C09C 4F04 6509 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 Spaces are for readability and not sent
frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: Specifies a FIR filter

VL_PROPERTY_C39_SHORT_MARGIN Bit5: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE. VL_PROPERTY_EAN_SHORT_MARGIN (if bytes[1]-[2] == 0) Bit6: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE. VL_PROPERTY_MSI_SHORT_MARGIN Bit7: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE. VL_PROPERTY_25IND_SHORT_MARGIN Byte[1]-[2]: 16 bit value VL_PROPERTY_EAN_CODE_QUIET_ZONE Byte[3]-[4]: 16 bit value VL_PROPERTY_EAN_ADDON_QUIET_ZONE
frs_context.notes: Consult Vision Library documentation for more information on these properties.

frs_context.description: Override margin control for EAN, C128, C39 short margin Byte 0 EAN short margin control Byte 1 C128 short margin control Byte 2 C39 short margin control See notes for settings.
frs_context.notes: If set greater than 0 overrides control in CI_MARGIN_CONTROL for EAN, C128, C39 short margin control. 0 = Normal quiet zone, 1 = short quiet zone, 2 = no quiet zone. If set above 2 the value is ignored and default is used. If configured value is set to greater than 0 priority is given to this tag for the label type and CI_MARGIN_CONTROL is ignored. If CI_MARGIN_CONTROL is set and the value in this tag is 0 the old tag value is used, maintaining backwards compatibility. If neither is greater than 0 the property is set to 0 which is default.

frs_context.description: Selects the minimum read algorithm.
frs_context.notes: This configuration item allows the user to select which minimum read algorithm to use to improve data security. Before reporting label data to a host, the Consecutive Minimum Read algorithm requires the scanner to read the same Label data a consecutive number of times. The Voting Minimum Read algorithm requires the scanner to read the same label data a minimum number of times but the label data does not have to be read consecutively. The Voting Minimum Read Algorithm is less secure than the Consecutive Minimum Read algorithm but allows incorrect data to be discarded without Discarding the accumulated reads of the correct data. The number of reads that are required is configurable based on symbology type and is set using CI_VL_MINIMUM_READ_CONTROL and CI_XXX_MINIMUM_READ where XXX is the symbology type. Both the Consecutive Minimum Read algorithm and the Voting Minimum Read Algorithm make use of the CI_MINIMUM_READ_ADDER.
frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: The number of additional required reads when more than one string of label data is decoded during a session to read a single barcode.
frs_context.notes: This configuration value is added to the symbology specific minimum number of reads required when more than one string of Label data is decoded during a session to read a single barcode. When more than one string is detected, the required reads Becomes the sum of the configuration item value and the symbology specific minimum read setting. This helps to increase the Data security when there are multiple results while attempting to decode of a poor quality label.
frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: Controls the Vision Library minimum read control setting for numerous symbologies.
frs_context.notes: Overrides the default minimum reads behavior. The first three bytes are used to enable frame based minimum reads. Each of the bits is used to indicate if the related CI_XXX_MINIMUM_READ item uses frame based minimum reads. BYTE 0 (left most byte): Bit 0 = UPCA Bit 1 = UPCE Bit 2 = EAN8 Bit 3 = EAN13 Bit 4 = not used (P2_ADDON VSL only) Bit 5 = not used (P5_ADDON VSL only) Bit 6 = INSTORE Bit 7 = EAN_TWO_LABEL BYTE 1: Bit 0 = COD128 Bit 1 = CODE39 Bit 2 = CODE93 Bit 3 = CODABAR Bit 4 = I2OF5 Bit 5 = S25 Bit 6 = MSI Bit 7 = reserved BYTE 2: Bit 0 = DATABAR_14 Bit 1 = DATABAR_EXPANDED Bit 2 thru Bit 7 = reserved Modify minimum read behavior for specific data patterns. BYTE 3: Bit 0 = MINIMUM_READ+1 for special patterns Bit 1 = MINIMUM_READ+1 for UPCEAN ambiguous characters Bit 2 thru Bit 7 = reserved

CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_TRANSMIT_MODE.context: GS1 Digital Link Set Transmit Mode

frs_context.parameter: GS1 Digital Link Set Transmit Mode
frs_context.description: GS1 Digital Link Set Transmit Mode
frs_context.notes: 00=Disabled, do not look for GS1 Label Set 01=Send first decoded label of the GS1 Label Set, discard other labels of the set read within the timeout specified by CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_TIMEOUT 03=Give priority to GS1 2D Label, if 2D label not seen within time specified by CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_TIMEOUT, send the DWM or 1D label if read. If both DWM and 1D labels read, the DWM label will be transmitted. 02=Send all decoded labels decoded and prepend label data with Label Set Number [USBOEM interface transmits labels as unknown label type]
frs_context.head: GS1 Digital Link

frs_context.description: Formatting options for GS1 2D and digital watermark labels
frs_context.notes: For conversion options 03 and 04, the simplest (smallest) of the listed symbologies that can completely contain the GTIN data will be selected as the conversion target. For conversion option 03, even if the label is compatible with EAN8, it will be sent as EAN13 and even if the label is compatible with UPCE, it will be sent as UPCA. 1D labels will be sent in their original label format.
frs_context.head: GS1 Digital Link

frs_context.description: Time to monitor for GS1 Digital Link set labels.

CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_DOUBLE_READ_TIME.context: GS1 Digital Link Double Read Timeout

frs_context.parameter: GS1 Digital Link Double Read Timeout
frs_context.description: Specifies minimum allowable time between consecutive reads of the same GS1 Digital Link Set. Any labels seen after timeout specified by CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_TIMEOUT has expired will not be transmitted until after this timeout has expired.
frs_context.notes: In normal usage the double read timer should always be set higher than 01. Due to the way the software handles the timer the actual timer value (when set to 1) is between 0 mS and 5mS. A settings of 1 is generally used for testing purposes only.
frs_context.head: GS1 Digital Link

frs_context.parameter: GS1 Digital Link Label Set ID
frs_context.description: Field identifier for the Label Set Number that is transmitted before the label data
frs_context.notes: The label ID is 4 bytes. The first 3 bytes are characters for the label ID. A 00 (hex) value in the first 3 bytes indicates the end of the label ID characters. Only active when CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_TRANSMIT_MODE = 02, Send all labels.

frs_context.description: Used during IPE initialization to define the IPE's software feature set.
frs_context.notes: Engineering Only, nu=not used, dcdr=decoder, lbl=label Used @ IPE init overrides behaviors & other config settings in deflt IPE when bits set 16 byte hex str divided into 2 groups of 4 16-bit entries CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP used to assoc feature set & override enble with IPE Default BIT 0&4=vrt 1&5=nu 2&6=nu 3&7=nu Index into array defined as contents of CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15] Ex: array[CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15]] entry is used I.e. CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15]=1 then this array[1] controls setting 1st 4 entries set feature enable/disable for IPE 0=VL 1D dcdr 1=VL 2D dcdr 2=VSL dcdr 3=Img capt/compress 4=Obj distance measure-JADE 5=VL 2D internal oneshot mode 6=Digimarc dcdr 2nd 4 entries enable override of default for IPE 0=VL 1D dcdr 1=VL 2D dcdr 2=nu 3=Img capt/compress 4=nu 5=VL 2D internal oneshot mode 6=nu If enable bit not set default used Ex: Override V & H IPEs to read 1D&2D lbl -spaces for readability 0003 0003 0000 0000 0023 0023 0000 0000
frs_context.head: Image Processing Engine (IPE)

frs_context.description: Selects the IPE's 1D VL locator pattern
frs_context.notes: Selects type of 1D VL locator pattern. Each entry is 32-bit big endian enumerated value (upper 16-bits must be zeros and are reserved). An entry are mapped to a specific IPE using the CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP tag. Enumerated values: 00000000=default (full frame) 00000001=Horizontal split 00000002=Vertical split 00000003=Top split (NA) 00000004=Jade area mode The index into this array is defined as the contents of CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15] Example Array[CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15]] entry is used I.e. CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15]=1 then this array[1] controls the setting.
frs_context.head: Image Processing Engine (IPE)

frs_context.description: Selects the type of color filter pattern
frs_context.notes: Select the type of color filter pattern: 00 - None (monochrome imager) 01 - Bayer (color imager)
frs_context.head: Image Processing Engine (IPE)

frs_context.description: Maps the hw id of an IPE to the imager hw configuration.
frs_context.notes: Each IPE contains a hardware ID range 0 thru 0x0F. This 16 word table is indexed by the hardware ID and it is used to map that id into a mode on how the imager is to be configured. The Null mode is used to configure the IPE to not use an imager, effectively turning off that imager. The mapping of imagers 0 = Vertical, 1 = Horizontal, 2 = 2D processor, 3 = reserved - do not use F = Null imager (non-attached) Table index 15 (0x0F) is the single entry used for the 1500i, all other entries have no effect. Other configuration items derive the index from this array entry; CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15] If CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP[15] == 1 then the value is used as an index into the other configuration item array.
frs_context.head: Image Processing Engine (IPE)

frs_context.notes: Delay of the illumination for the imager. Disregard the tag description-- this controls the illumination delay on all imagers. This represents the delay of the illumination in 1 us steps. Default is 32us (0x001E) First 16 bits is for the imager Second 16 bits - reserved - do not use

frs_context.description: Image processing parameters for the IPEs
frs_context.notes: IPE tag expansion, 16 bytes=array, four items, ea item=32-bit(4 byte) val=big endian format CI_IPE_IMAGER_ASSOCIATION_MAP associates item value with IPE Nu=not used, B=Byte, b=bit Default item map 0=V 1=H 2=2D proc 3=nu B0 right most(least sig.) of ea item B3-0 adv decode modes 0000=std decoder 0001=UPC albatross correction 0010=linear albatross correction 0100=nu 1000=nu B1 defines B0=enable saturation detection B2-1=linear locator LUT type 00=Comprss 01=nu 10=Linear 11=nu B3=enable override default linear locator edge meth B7-4=linear loc edge methods 0001=meth 111 0010=meth 161 0100=meth 141 1000=meth FAST1 B2 defines B1-0=V scanline pickmode 00=dma pick=deflt 01=sw pick 10=hw pick 11=nu B3-2=scan line filt 00=Lowpass filt 01=nofilt 10=nofilt 11=noflt B7-4=nu B3 ROI & override config B3-0=ipe features (b: 0=1D, 1=2D, 2=VSL, 3=image proc) WARNING: feature override not compatible with Cell Mode on H/V IPEs and DWM on 2D IPE B7-4=ROI 0=full,1=top,2=bottom
frs_context.head: Image Processing Engine (IPE)

frs_context.head: Advanced Decoder

frs_context.description: Enable or disable improving Datamatrix fast decode
frs_context.head: Advanced Decoder

frs_context.description: Select the operating mode of the Datamatrix Decoder.
frs_context.notes: From the VL Documentation: Select the operating mode of the Datamatrix Decoder. The Robust and the Very robust modes are for DPM applications. The main philosophy is that FAST modes will try to decode or reject as quick as possible, while ROBUST modes will try to fix distorted codes and correct the graph as much as possible until it is decodeable. No effect on the amount or use of Reed-Solomon error correction, only the construction of the graph. Faster operating modes may decode some labels faster but will give up on difficult to read labels. Robust operating modes may decode some labels slower but will decode difficult to read labels.
frs_context.head: Advanced Decoder

frs_context.description: Changes the case of all alphabetic characters to upper or lower case.
frs_context.head: Data Format

frs_context.description: Converts transmission characters from one value to another.
frs_context.notes: There are 8 characters in this conversion string: Characters [1] [2] [3] [4] [5] [6] [7] [8]. The following conversions occur: Character [1] is converted to Character [2]; If Character [1] is 0xFF, there is no conversion. Character [3] is converted to Character [4]; If Character [3] is 0xFF, there is no conversion. Character [5] is converted to Character [6]; If Character [5] is 0xFF, there is no conversion. Character [7] is converted to Character [8]; If Character [7] is 0xFF, there is no conversion.

frs_context.description: Describes the coordinates for the Region Of Interest Byte [0]-[1]: 16 bit hex value xmin Byte [2]-[3]: 16 bit hex value xmax Byte [4]-[5]: 16 bit hex value ymin Byte [6]-[7]: 16 bit hex value ymax
frs_context.notes: Coordinate values must follow these rules: 0 <= xmin < 1280 0 <= xmax < 1280 Xmin < xmax 0 <= ymin < 800 0 <= ymax < 800 Ymin < ymax If the xmax/ymax values are configured larger than the maximum values above, they will default to 1 less than their respective maximum values. If the xmin/ymin values are configured larger than xmax/ymax, they will default to 0. Each independent value is in units of pixels.

frs_context.notes: Only to be set by engineering. Reserved
frs_context.head: Developer

frs_context.description: This configuration items allows certain testing features to be enabled or disabled.
frs_context.notes: NOTE: Bit 3 and Bit 7 of this configuration item is for SW development only and should not be tested by SW Test This allows the software to be used by SW test to control certain operation in the scanner. The default value of this configuration item as built is zero. Bit 0 : IPC speed 1=750KHz, 0=12MHz Bit 1 : 0=UI Heartbeat enabled, 1= UI Heartbeat disabled Bit 2 : 1=Linux USB testport support enabled Bit 3: 1=double buffering on 0=double buffer off Bit 4 : 1= Continuous ORT Startup Memtest - memtests run until button press. Bit 5 : 1= UI Heartbeat @ 10Hz Bit 6 : 1= Statistics update every 10 sec, button debounce 20ms Bit 7 : 1= replicate legacy CI_NUM_HOST_TX_BUFFERS 0= 1 buffer 1=2 buffers
frs_context.head: Developer

frs_context.description: Config item to allow software development to put device under test into specific mode.
frs_context.notes: NOTE: This configuration item is for SW development only and should not be tested by SW Test Bit 7 - scale reset allowed on stuck below zero condition Bit 6 - allow forbidden files to be copied from SD card to internal drive. Bit 4 & Bit 5 00= normal 2D ipe operation, default 01= use the 2D ipe as a 1D processor. 10= configure V and 2D as both 1D and 2D decoder 11 = configure V and 2D as dynamic 1D and 2D decoders Bit 3 - Enable EAS port use as debug port Bit 2 - not used Bit 1 - (=1)Enable SC reset to use jump to cstartup vs. doing a warm reset. Necessary for Emulation. Bit 0 - (=1) Enable IPE debug capability. Bypasses the strict restart of the ipe and extends timeouts. Used for emulation
frs_context.head: Developer

frs_context.description: Config item to allow software development to put device under test into specific mode.
frs_context.notes: Bit 0: (==1)disable hardware watchdog Bit 1 : (==1) non-null UI reset awaits button press to reset Bit 2: (==1) turns on extended label POS output as C128 label Bit 3: (==1)bypass any reset of comet device, turn off heartbeat, beep after reset sequence Bit 4: (==1)force UI monitor mode entry Bit 5: ==1 sets EAS error limit to 1 before considered fatal (good for debugging) Bit 6: ==1 Enables EAS event logging Bit 7: ==1 Turns off User override for EAS failures
frs_context.head: Developer

frs_context.description: Config item to allow software test to put device under test into specific mode

Bit 6: 1 = disable fast spi support Bit 7: 1 = Resets Configuration Check
